excerpt:
Rocky Mountain Area(PL 4)   
Fire Activity and Teams Assigned Totals 
New fires: 35 
New large incidents: 1 
Uncontained large fires: 8 
CIMTs Committed: 5 
 
Aspen Acres, Custer County. CIMT (NR Team 6). Twenty miles southwest of Pueblo, CO. Timber. Extreme 
fire behavior with short crown runs, uphill runs and short-range spotting. Numerous residences and 
communication infrastructure threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
Fishhook, Medicine Bow-Routt NF, USFS. CIMT (SW Team 1). Eight miles southeast of Steamboat Springs, 
CO. Grass, brush and timber. Minimal fire behavior with creeping, group torching and smoldering. Structures 
and energy infrastructure threatened. Area, road and trail closures in effect. Reduction in acreage due to more 
accurate mapping. 
 
Gold Mountain, Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre and Gunnison NF, USFS. CIMT (RM Team 2). Two miles north 
of Ouray, CO. Grass, brush and timber. Moderate fire behavior with flanking, backing and torching. Numerous 
structures and critical infrastructure threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
Elk, Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre and Gunnison NF, USFS. CIMT (NW Team 8). Seven miles northwest of 
Lake City, CO. Timber, heavy slash and grass. Minimal fire behavior with flanking, backing and isolated 
torching. Numerous structures threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
Willow, Pike and San Isabel NF, USFS. CIMT (CA Team 8). Six miles west of Leadville, CO. Brush, closed 
timber litter and timber. Moderate fire behavior with flanking, isolated torching and spotting. Residences and 
critical infrastructure threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
Ferris, San Juan NF, USFS. Seven miles east of Cahone, CO. Timber and brush. Minimal fire behavior with 
creeping and smoldering. Area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
P L Gulch, White River Field Office, BLM. Nineteen miles southwest of Meeker, CO. Brush, grass and timber. 
Moderate fire behavior with backing, creeping and smoldering. Structures and energy infrastructure 
threatened. 
 
* Big Gulch, Moffat County. Eight miles northwest of Craig, CO. Grass and brush. Active fire behavior with 
running and torching.
